creator: nakaji@jp.FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi, Timur,

The difference from samba33 is,

o PORTNAME includes version information (PORTNAME=samba34)
o swat is enabled
o shared libraries are built (WITH_SHARED_LIBS=yes)
o some files/patch-* are removed
o and some tiny changes

Of cource, I'm not sure about

o if swat works properly
o if the installed shared libralies do not have confliction with other
  packages

The reason I removed some patches is

o some changes are imported in samba-3.4, for example,
  - samba-3.4.5/source3/../nsswitch/winbind_nss_freebsd.c is available
o some patches cannot be applied
  - but I forgot what cannot be applied, sorry
o I do not fully understand the meanings of samba33/files/patch-*, I
  just tried "to make install/deinstall"

I put "diff -urNb samba33 samba34" at
http://www.heimat.gr.jp/~nakaji/FreeBSD/ports/net/samba33-34.diff

I'll check again my update. Thanks.

>>>>> In <201001221654.o0MGsEHe063898@freefall.freebsd.org> 
>>>>>	timur@FreeBSD.org wrote:

> As I see, this port is a slightly modified version of samba33.

> Can you, please, give more details what non-trivial changes
> were done and why certain patches from 33 were dropped?

> I'm trying to merge all the effords for this port to happen,
> so have to deal with changes only.
-- 
NAKAJI Hiroyuki
